verdict_reasoning: HSPD1 (HSP60) is canonically a mitochondrial matrix chaperonin. However, it is well-documented to translocate to the cell surface under stress, oncogenic transformation, and immunogenic/apoptotic conditions. Surface HSP60 has been detected on tumor cells and stressed cells by flow cytometry and surface biotinylation, where it acts as a danger signal and target for NK cells and antibodies. Several antibody and CAR-T programs have been directed against cell-surface HSP60 on cancer cells. This qualifies as cell_state_induced contextual surface accessibility.
